senses.definition: processed or sifted beforehand, typically to remove larger particles before final sorting
examples: The presifted flour reduces the need for additional sieving.
Presifted seeds ensure uniform planting.

senses.definition: the act of sifting something beforehand to remove chunks or impurities
examples: Presifting flour helps create a lighter batter.
Presifting gravel improves drainage.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of presift meaning sifts beforehand
examples: The recipe presifts the dry ingredients to avoid lumps.
He presifts the sand before mixing.

senses.definition: to sign something in advance or to place a sign before an event or action
examples: The contract was presigned by both parties.
Presign the document to expedite approval.

senses.definition: an advance signal or preliminary indication of something to come
examples: A presignal indicated the storm was approaching.
The red flag acted as a presignal of danger.

senses.definition: to signal in advance
examples: The alarms presignaled an exit route.
These indicators presign the outcome.

senses.definition: past tense of presignal; signaled in advance
examples: The warnings presignaled the need to evacuate.
The system presignaled a maintenance window.

senses.definition: the act of signaling in advance; the process of giving a presignal
examples: Presignaling can help organize emergency responses.
Presignaling may reduce reaction times.

senses.definition: present participle of presignal; signaling in advance
examples: The system is presignaling an alert.
Presignaling helps crews prepare.

senses.definition: past tense of presignalled; British English variant of presignaled
examples: The warning was presignalled to the team.
The document was presignalled before release.

senses.definition: the act of signaling in advance; the process of giving a presignal
examples: Presignalling is used in aviation to prepare crews.
Presignalling can improve safety margins.

senses.definition: present participle of presignal; signaling in advance
examples: The system is presignalling an alert.
Presignalling helps coordinate teams.

senses.definition: past tense of presignaling; signaled in advance (British English)
examples: Warnings were presignalled to all departments.
The project was presignalled before the kickoff.

senses.definition: the act of signaling in advance; the process of giving a presignal
examples: Presignalling reduces risk by giving early notice.
Presignalling is common in air traffic control.

senses.definition: present participle of presignal; signaling in advance
examples: The system is presignalling an alert.
Presignalling helps crews prepare.

senses.definition: see presignalled (British English past tense of presignaling)
examples: The warning was presignalled to staff.
Procedures were presignalled to ensure smooth evacuation.

senses.definition: see presignalling (process of signaling in advance)
examples: Presignalling is used in safety protocols.
The presignalling phase begins early.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of presignal; signals in advance
examples: The device presignals danger to the operator.
These indicators presignals an upcoming change.

senses.definition: the quality or state of being presignificant; having significance beforehand
examples: The presignificance of the data was debated.
Presignificance can guide early decisions.

senses.definition: an archaic or rare term meaning the state or quality of being presignificant
examples: The theory hinges on presignificancy.
Discussing presignificancy requires careful terminology.

senses.definition: having significance in advance; indicating something important before it happens
examples: Early warnings are presignificant.
Presignificant signals guided the response.

senses.definition: the act or process of giving fore-significance; the state of being presignified
examples: Presignification is a theoretical concept in linguistics.
They studied presignification across languages.

senses.definition: having the quality of presignification; relating to fore-significance
examples: A presignificative approach considers future impact.
The term is used in philosophy texts.

senses.definition: an agent or thing that signs or signals in advance; a forecaster
examples: The presignificator alerted teams early.
As a presignificator, the system flags upcoming issues.

senses.definition: past tense of presignify; to have been made presignificant
examples: The event was presignified in the report.
They presignified the outcome to stakeholders.

senses.definition: third-person singular present of presignify; signifies in advance
examples: The model presignifies potential risks.
The chart presignifies upcoming trends.

senses.definition: to signify in advance; to indicate beforehand
examples: These data presignify a potential shift.
The warning presignifies a change in policy.

senses.definition: present participle of presignify; signaling in advance
examples: The system is presignifying a potential alert.
Presignifying indicators help mitigation planning.

senses.definition: unnatural or archaic term; historically used to describe forelimb-like features in primates; in modern usage not common
examples: The presimian fossil showed unusual anatomy.

senses.definition: the period or process before slaughter, often in meat production contexts
examples: Preslaughter handling affects meat quality.
Farmers monitor preslaughter conditions closely.

senses.definition: historical or literary term referring to an era or attitudes before slavery
examples: Scholars discuss preslavery in early colonial contexts.

senses.definition: the period just before sleep; bedtime
examples: He read a book during presleep quiet.
A warm drink helps during presleep routine.

senses.definition: a surname or given name; most famously associated with Elvis Presley
examples: Elvis Presley was a legendary musician.
